Veqtor v0.1.2 is a public-Alpha hardening release for external installation
and bounded local DOCX processing.

Highlights:

- one-line, version-pinned Claude Code registration through PyPI and `uvx`;
- MCP initialization reports the Veqtor product version;
- one shared fail-closed DOCX boundary that accepts only unencrypted
  `STORED`/`DEFLATED` ZIP members, reconciles security-relevant local, central
  and descriptor fields, and bounds actual expansion while verifying CRC and
  DEFLATE end-of-stream;
- bounded parsed XML elements, extracted change units, round-folder scans and
  atomic edit batches, plus XML parsing that refuses DTDs, custom entity
  expansion and unbounded numbering labels;
- raised safe minimum dependency versions;
- clearer Claude scopes, support expectations, security reporting and public
  product identity;
- the protected exact tag is durably reserved first; the same approved wheel
  and sdist are then published to PyPI through OIDC Trusted Publishing and
  publicly verified before the matching GitHub Release becomes immutable.

Veqtor remains a local, community-supported technical Alpha for macOS and
Linux with Python 3.12-3.14. It is not legal advice, a hosted service, a full
Word editor or a tamper-evident audit system. Review the
[known limitations](https://github.com/JohnDeer-ai/veqtor-mcp/blob/v0.1.2/KNOWN_LIMITATIONS.md)
before using it on a real matter.
